title: gladiopappus vernonioides
intro: Gladiopappus vernonioides, also known as the globe daisy, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to Australia and New Zealand. The leaves and flowers of the globe daisy have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, while also acting as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Globe daisy is often prepared for medicinal use in the form of infusions, teas, and tinctures, allowing for a range of herbal preparations to suit individual needs.
